title: Oil Surges on Iran-US Escalation, Russia Bans Aviation Fuel Exports
subhead: Brent crude jumped above $93 as Iran suspended nuclear talks and threatened Hormuz closure, while Russia banned aviation fuel exports through November and OPEC+ signaled another production hike despite Middle East supply risks.

sections.heading: Iran Suspends Talks, Threatens Hormuz Closure
sections.body:
Iran halted indirect negotiations with the United States and threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz, accusing Washington of violating ceasefire commitments as Israel deepened its offensive in Lebanon. The Tasnim news agency reported Tehran is stopping message exchanges with Washington, while Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned that any breach of the ceasefire would nullify the Iran-US truce. Brent crude rose more than $3 to above $93 per barrel on the news.

The suspension came as Iranian missiles struck a US base in Kuwait, injuring seven people according to Anadolu Agency, while US forces intercepted two Iranian ballistic missiles targeting American positions. The escalation marks the most serious breakdown in US-Iran negotiations since the three-month conflict began, though both sides indicated talks have not permanently collapsed.
sections.whyItMatters: The Hormuz threat and talk suspension inject new risk premium into oil markets precisely as OPEC+ prepares to raise output. If negotiations fail to resume, roughly 20% of global oil supply could face disruption, testing US security guarantees in the Gulf.

sections.heading: Russia Bans Aviation Fuel Exports Through November
sections.body:
The Russian government banned aviation fuel exports until November 30 as Ukrainian drone attacks continue to strike Russian refineries and energy infrastructure. Reuters reports the export restriction aims to secure domestic supply after months of sustained Ukrainian strikes on Russian fuel production facilities.

Ukraine has intensified its campaign against Russian energy sites, targeting refineries, depots, and pipeline infrastructure. A separate Reuters report documented Ukrainian drone strikes overnight hitting an oil pipeline pumping station, a refinery, and a fuel depot across multiple Russian regions. The aviation fuel ban signals Moscow's concern over wartime supply constraints.
sections.whyItMatters: Russia's aviation fuel export ban reveals the cumulative impact of Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure. While the ban affects a niche product, it demonstrates how drone warfare is constraining Russia's export capacity and forcing defensive measures.

sections.heading: OPEC+ to Raise July Output Despite Middle East Disruption
sections.body:
OPEC+ countries will likely agree to a further increase in their output target for July when they meet on Sunday, Reuters reports, citing three sources familiar with the matter. The decision comes despite Iran-related disruption risks, though several OPEC+ members have struggled to deliver previous production increases amid the ongoing Middle East conflict.

The planned hike suggests the cartel sees sufficient spare capacity to offset potential Hormuz disruptions, but the gap between targets and actual delivery highlights production challenges across the alliance. Saudi Arabia and the UAE have shouldered most of the burden of previous increases.
sections.whyItMatters: OPEC+ is attempting to balance competing pressures: prevent price spikes from Iranian escalation while acknowledging that some members cannot boost output. The decision tests whether the alliance can manage crisis-level geopolitics while maintaining market stability.

sections.heading: France Seizes Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker in Atlantic
sections.body:
French naval forces boarded and seized a Russia-linked oil tanker in international waters in the Atlantic, President Emmanuel Macron announced. The vessel, identified by Reuters as the Tagor, was accused of flying a false flag in violation of EU sanctions on Russian oil exports. UK helicopter support participated in the operation.

Russia condemned the seizure as international piracy, according to France 24, while Macron demanded the unconditional reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and called for a rapid US-Iran agreement. The interception marks France's third seizure of a sanctioned Russian oil tanker, intensifying sanctions enforcement in European waters.
sections.whyItMatters: European sanctions enforcement is escalating from financial restrictions to physical interdiction of shadow fleet vessels. The seizure demonstrates EU resolve but risks Russian retaliation and tests the limits of enforcement against the expanding shadow fleet.

sections.heading: BP Starts Gas Production at Azerbaijan's ACG Field
sections.body:
BP began commercial production of non-associated gas at the Azeri-Chirag-Gunashli (ACG) field off Azerbaijan's coast, Reuters reports. The expansion adds new supply to the Southern Gas Corridor, which delivers Caspian gas to Europe via Turkey, reducing continental reliance on Russian pipeline imports.

Separately, Trend.az reports that Azerbaijan and US energy major Chevron discussed energy export options via the Caspian Sea, including new transportation routes and decarbonization projects. The talks signal continued Western interest in Caspian energy infrastructure as an alternative to Russian supply.
sections.whyItMatters: New Caspian gas production strengthens Europe's energy diversification away from Russia, while US engagement with Azerbaijan on export routes suggests continued investment in alternative supply corridors despite regional tensions.

sections.heading: Turkey-Azerbaijan Plan Electricity Corridor
sections.body:
Turkey and Azerbaijan are expanding their energy partnership beyond oil and gas into electricity transmission and green energy corridors, Turkish Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar told Reuters. The plan builds on the TANAP gas pipeline model to create a regional electricity interconnection.

The initiative reflects Ankara's ambition to position Turkey as a regional energy transit hub for both fossil fuels and renewable power, connecting Caspian producers to European markets. Turkey's strategic location between major producers and consumers gives it leverage in energy transit negotiations.
sections.whyItMatters: Turkey is leveraging its geographic position to diversify energy diplomacy beyond hydrocarbons, creating new infrastructure that could eventually rival Russian electricity exports and support European energy security.

sections.heading: China Crude Imports Slump to Near-Decade Low
sections.body:
China's seaborne crude imports fell to the lowest in almost a decade in May, Reuters reports, as the Iran war reshuffled operations for the world's biggest oil importer. The decline reflects both economic slowdown and the impact of Middle East supply chain disruptions on Chinese refiners.

The import slump suggests Chinese demand is responding to higher prices and supply uncertainty, potentially limiting global oil price upside even as Iran-US tensions escalate. Reuters analysis notes the drop is driven by economics rather than political restraint.
sections.whyItMatters: Weakening Chinese crude demand creates a counterweight to Middle East supply concerns. If Chinese imports remain depressed, the demand-side shock could offset some of the risk premium from Hormuz-related disruption fears.

bottomLine: Oil markets entered June with a new risk premium as Iran suspended nuclear talks and threatened Hormuz closure, pushing Brent above $93. Russia's aviation fuel export ban revealed the strain from Ukrainian strikes on its energy infrastructure, while France seized a third Russian shadow fleet tanker in the Atlantic. OPEC+ signaled confidence with plans to raise July output despite Middle East uncertainty, and BP launched new Caspian gas production that strengthens Europe's supply diversification. The day's developments show energy weaponization intensifying on multiple fronts—Hormuz threats, sanctions enforcement, and infrastructure strikes—all competing with weaker Chinese demand to set prices.
sources: Reuters, Al-Monitor, Anadolu Agency, France 24, Trend.az, BBC, The Guardian, AP News, SCMP, Yahoo Finance, Bloomberg, Channels TV, RFI
